description: Revision for networking, cybersecurity, and internet concepts.

topics.summary: DNS translates human-readable domain names into IP addresses so browsers can load websites.

notes.heading: What DNS does
notes.content: DNS stands for Domain Name System. It works like the internet's phonebook by turning names like google.com into IP addresses.

notes.heading: Why DNS matters
notes.content: Without DNS, users would need to remember long IP addresses instead of simple website names.

flashcards.question: What is the main purpose of DNS?
flashcards.answer: To translate domain names into IP addresses.

quizQuestions.question: What does DNS translate?
quizQuestions.explanation: DNS maps readable website names to IP addresses.

glossary.definition: A system that translates domain names into IP addresses.
